title: Form 36 — Affidavit for Divorce
subtitle: Ontario Family Court — Family Law Rules (FLR 36)
helpIntro: Form 36 is the sworn affidavit you file to support your divorce application. It proves to the court that the legal requirements for a divorce are met — mainly that you have been separated for at least one year and that there is no reasonable chance of reconciliation. This form is sworn before a commissioner of oaths.

parts.title: Court information
parts.subtitle: Step 1 of 7
parts.intro: Enter the court file details from your divorce application.

fields.label: Courthouse
options: St. Catharines — Superior Court of Justice
Thunder Bay — Superior Court of Justice

fields.label: Court file number
Applicant's full legal name
Respondent's full legal name

parts.title: The marriage
parts.subtitle: Step 2 of 7
parts.intro: Provide details about your marriage. This information is needed to verify that a valid marriage took place.

fields.label: Date of marriage
City or town where you were married
Province or country where you were married
Do you have a marriage certificate?
fields.helpText: You must file a certified copy of your marriage certificate with the court. If you don't have one, contact ServiceOntario or the vital statistics office where you were married.

fields.label: If you do not have a marriage certificate, explain why
fields.placeholder: e.g. We were married in another country and have been unable to obtain a certified copy. We are in the process of requesting one from the registry office.

fields.label: Your full name at the time of marriage (if different from current name)
fields.placeholder: Leave blank if your name has not changed

fields.label: Other party's full name at the time of marriage (if different from current name)
fields.placeholder: Leave blank if their name has not changed

parts.title: The separation
parts.subtitle: Step 3 of 7
parts.intro: The Divorce Act requires that spouses have lived separate and apart for at least one year before a divorce can be granted. Answer the questions below to confirm this requirement is met.

fields.label: Date you and your spouse separated
fields.helpText: This is the date one or both of you decided the marriage was over and began living separate lives — even if you remained in the same house.

fields.label: Have you lived separate and apart for at least one year as of today?
fields.helpText: If you separated less than one year ago, you cannot apply for a divorce yet. You must wait until the full year has passed.

fields.label: Did you and your spouse attempt to reconcile after separating?
fields.helpText: Short periods of attempted reconciliation (totalling 90 days or less) do not reset the one-year clock.

fields.label: Describe the reconciliation attempt(s)
fields.placeholder: e.g. We reconciled from April 1 to April 30, 2024 (30 days). We then separated again permanently on May 1, 2024.
fields.helpText: Include dates. If total reconciliation attempts were 90 days or less combined, your one-year clock continues from the original separation date.

fields.label: Is there any reasonable chance you and your spouse will reconcile?
fields.helpText: Almost always 'No' — the court needs this confirmed to grant the divorce.

fields.label: Did you and your spouse live in the same home after separating?
fields.helpText: It is possible to be 'separated' while living under the same roof if you were living separate lives (separate bedrooms, separate finances, no intimate relationship).

fields.label: Describe how you lived separately while in the same home
fields.placeholder: e.g. From March 1, 2024 we slept in separate bedrooms, had no intimate relationship, ate separately, managed our own finances, and informed family and friends that we were separated.
fields.helpText: The court needs specific details to accept an in-home separation.

parts.title: Children of the marriage
parts.subtitle: Step 4 of 7
parts.intro: Under the Divorce Act, the court must be satisfied that reasonable arrangements have been made for the support of any children of the marriage before granting a divorce.

fields.label: Do you have any children of the marriage under 18, or over 18 but still dependent?
fields.helpText: Children of the marriage include biological children, adopted children, and stepchildren who were treated as a child of the family.

fields.label: List the children (full name, date of birth, where they live now)
fields.placeholder:
Alex Lance — born April 12, 2015 — lives with Applicant at 123 Main St, Toronto
Sam Lance — born September 3, 2018 — lives with Applicant at 123 Main St, Toronto

fields.label: Have reasonable arrangements been made for child support?
fields.helpText: The court will not grant the divorce if it is not satisfied that proper support arrangements exist for the children.

fields.label: Describe the child support arrangements
fields.placeholder: e.g. The Respondent pays child support of $1,200 per month pursuant to the Child Support Guidelines based on an annual income of $72,000. This is set out in a separation agreement dated March 15, 2025.
fields.helpText: Reference any existing court orders, separation agreements, or FRO arrangements.

fields.label: Describe the current parenting / custody arrangement
fields.placeholder: e.g. The children live primarily with the Applicant. The Respondent has parenting time every other weekend pursuant to a separation agreement dated March 15, 2025.

parts.title: Collusion and condonation
parts.subtitle: Step 5 of 7
parts.intro: The court must be satisfied that the divorce is not the result of any agreement to deceive the court, and that no matrimonial offence has been condoned. These are standard legal declarations.

fields.label: I confirm that there has been no collusion in relation to this divorce application
fields.helpText: Collusion means a secret agreement to deceive the court — for example, fabricating grounds for divorce or agreeing to hide the truth. This is extremely rare in modern no-fault divorces based on separation.

fields.label: I confirm that I have not condoned any conduct that might be raised as a ground for divorce
fields.helpText: Condonation means forgiving a matrimonial offence (like adultery or cruelty) and resuming the marriage with full knowledge of it. For separation-based divorces, this is generally not applicable but must be declared.

fields.label: Grounds for divorce
options: Separation — we have lived separate and apart for at least one year (most common)
Physical or mental cruelty — my spouse treated me with physical or mental cruelty
fields.helpText: Almost all divorces in Canada are granted on the grounds of separation. Adultery and cruelty are rarely used because they are harder to prove and require additional evidence.

fields.label: If claiming adultery — describe the circumstances
fields.placeholder: e.g. My spouse committed adultery with a person known to me from approximately January 2024 onwards.
fields.helpText: You do not need to name the third party, but you must have sufficient evidence. Consider whether to proceed on this ground vs. separation.

fields.label: If claiming cruelty — describe the conduct

conditional.showWhen: Physical or mental cruelty — my spouse treated me with physical or mental cruelty

fields.placeholder: e.g. From 2022 to 2024, my spouse subjected me to physical violence on multiple occasions, including...
fields.helpText: Must be conduct that made continued cohabitation intolerable. Provide specific incidents with dates.

parts.title: Previous court proceedings
parts.subtitle: Step 6 of 7
parts.intro: The court needs to know if there are any other divorce or family law proceedings anywhere in Canada or internationally.

fields.label: Have there been any previous divorce or family law court proceedings between you and your spouse in any court?
Describe the previous proceedings
fields.placeholder: e.g. There is an existing Ontario Superior Court proceeding (File No. FC-2024-12345) which includes this divorce application. There are no other proceedings.
fields.helpText: Include the court, file number, and type of proceeding. Include proceedings in other provinces or countries.

fields.label: Do you have a signed separation agreement?
fields.helpText: If yes, attach a copy to your affidavit. The court will want to see how issues like support and property have been resolved.

fields.label: Date of the separation agreement
Does the separation agreement address the children?

parts.title: Declaration and signing
parts.subtitle: Step 7 of 7
parts.intro: This affidavit must be sworn or affirmed before a commissioner of oaths. Do not sign it until you are in front of the commissioner.

fields.label: Will you swear or affirm?
Municipality where you will sign this affidavit
Date of commissioning (leave blank to complete at courthouse)
I confirm that everything stated in this affidavit is true, and I understand that swearing a false affidavit is perjury.
